description: Add comprehensive table-driven unit tests for internal/util/decode.go, internal/hostname/hostname.go, internal/writefile/writefile.go, and internal/ssh/ssh.go (findUserHomeDir). These are all pure functions or interface-based functions testable with mocks.
steps: Create internal/util/decode_test.go with table-driven tests for DecodeBase64Content (valid base64, invalid base64, empty input), DecodeGzipContent (valid gzip string, invalid data), DecompressGzip (valid compressed bytes, invalid bytes, empty bytes), and DecodeContent (empty encoding passthrough, base64 variants, gzip variants, gz+base64 combined, unsupported encoding error). Use github.com/stretchr/testify/assert and require. Follow the table-driven pattern with t.Run sub-tests and t.Parallel() as seen in internal/cc/funcs_test.go.

Create internal/hostname/hostname_mock_test.go with MockFileSystem, MockFile, and MockHostnameSetter implementations following the exact pattern in internal/cc/filesystem_mock_test.go and internal/cc/hostname_mock_test.go. Include compile-time interface checks. The package declaration must be 'package hostname' (not 'package hostname_test') since syncHostname is unexported.

Create internal/hostname/hostname_test.go with table-driven tests for SetHostname (empty hostname no-op, SetHostname syscall error propagated, successful hostname set triggers syncHostname) and syncHostname (Hostname() returns empty string is no-op, full sync path writes /etc/hostname and updates 127.0.1.1 line in /etc/hosts, Hostname() error propagated, WriteFile /etc/hostname error propagated, Open /etc/hosts error propagated, hosts file with no 127.0.1.1 line preserves content). Use MockFileSystem, MockHostnameSetter, and MockFile from the mock file. Test syncHostname via SetHostname (since syncHostname is called after SetHostname succeeds).

Create internal/writefile/writefile_mock_test.go with MockFileSystem, MockFile, and MockCommandRunner implementations following the exact pattern in internal/cc/filesystem_mock_test.go and internal/cc/command_mock_test.go. Include compile-time interface checks. Also include a mockFileInfo struct (like in internal/cc/funcs_test.go) for Stat return values. Package declaration is 'package writefile'.

Create internal/writefile/writefile_test.go with tests for: ensureDirectoryExists (dir exists and is dir, path exists but is not dir returns error, Stat error triggers MkdirAll, MkdirAll error propagated), WriteFile (full success path with temp file create/write/close/chmod/rename, encoding not empty returns error, CreateTemp error, Write error, Close error, Chmod error, Rename error, owner field triggers cmd.Run chown), WriteFiles (multiple entries with one decode failure logs error and continues, empty list is no-op). Package declaration is 'package writefile' to access unexported ensureDirectoryExists.

Create internal/ssh/ssh_test.go with table-driven tests for findUserHomeDir: valid single user entry, multiple users finds correct one, user not found returns zero values, malformed passwd line (fewer than 6 fields), non-numeric uid returns error, non-numeric gid returns error, empty input returns zero values. Since findUserHomeDir is unexported, use package declaration 'package ssh'. Follow the table-driven pattern from internal/cc/funcs_test.go.
acceptance_criteria: go test ./internal/util/... passes with all new decode tests passing
go test ./internal/hostname/... passes with all new hostname tests passing
go test ./internal/writefile/... passes with all new writefile tests passing
go test ./internal/ssh/... passes with all new ssh tests passing
go test -coverprofile=coverage.out ./internal/util/... && go tool cover -func=coverage.out shows decode.go at 80%+ coverage
go test -coverprofile=coverage.out ./internal/hostname/... && go tool cover -func=coverage.out shows hostname.go at 80%+ coverage
go test -coverprofile=coverage.out ./internal/writefile/... && go tool cover -func=coverage.out shows writefile.go at 80%+ coverage
go test -coverprofile=coverage.out ./internal/ssh/... && go tool cover -func=coverage.out shows ssh.go findUserHomeDir function covered
